final_answer:
**Monday and Tuesday** will focus heavily on feature design and initial sprint planning meetings. The majority of development time on these days is dedicated to core coding and API integration tasks. Wednesday begins the hands-on testing phase for the implemented features.

Thursday allocates most of the day to bug fixing and deep unit testing across the new module. Friday morning is reserved for performance testing and final integration reviews. The afternoon features a comprehensive sprint retrospective to identify bottlenecks. Throughout the week, daily stand-up meetings (15 minutes) are mandatory for alignment and progress tracking. This structured approach ensures all phases, from coding to final testing, are completed efficiently within the timeline.<turn|>
